name: Plain Croissent
descr: layers of high-quality butter are folded repeatedly into a yeast-leavened dough.

name: Pain au Chocolat
descr: It’s square-shaped with two bars of dark chocolate inside.

name: Almond Croissant
descr: filled with almond cream, and topped with sliced almonds and powdered sugar.

name: Pistachio Croissant
descr: Filled with green pistachio cream and often dipped in white chocolate.

name: Lotus Biscoff Croissant
descr: Filled with Lotus spread and topped with crushed biscuits.

name: Nutella Croissant
descr: A classic safe choice — stuffed with hazelnut cocoa cream

name: Cheese Croissant
descr: Topped with melted cheddar or filled with salty white cheese/feta.

name: Turkey & Swiss
descr: smoked turkey and melted cheese inside.

name: Zaatar Croissant
descr: Flaky pastry topped or filled with a mix of thyme and olive oil.

name: The Cruffin
descr: A cross between a croissant and a muffin, usually filled with cream or jam.

name: The Cronut
descr: The famous donut-croissant hybrid, deep-fried and glazed.

name: New York Roll
descr: A circular, spiral-shaped croissant filled with heavy cream and topped with colorful ganache.

name: Matcha Croissant
descr: Filled with green tea custard, popular in East Asian bakeries.

name: Medialunas
descr: The Argentinian version—smaller, sweeter, and often brushed with a sugary glaze.

name: St. Martin’s Croissant
descr: A Polish specialty filled with white poppy seeds, nuts, and raisins.

name: Classic Chocolate Eclair
descr: Light choux pastry filled with silky vanilla pastry cream and glazed with rich dark chocolate ganache.

name: French Macarons
descr: A box of delicate almond meringue shells with various gourmet fillings like lavender, pistachio, and raspberry.

name: Vanilla Mille-Feuille
descr: Traditional French napoleon featuring layers of crispy puff pastry and smooth Madagascan vanilla cream.

name: San Sebastian Cheesecake
descr: A cheesecake with a creamy, molten center and a perfectly caramelized top.

name: Tiramisu Classico
descr: Authentic Italian dessert made of espresso-soaked ladyfingers and layers of whipped mascarpone cream.

name: Pistachio Stuffed Cookies
descr: Thick, soft-baked cookies loaded with white chocolate chips and a gooey pistachio cream center.

name: Chocolate Fudge Brownie
descr: Rich, chewy brownies made with premium cocoa and chunks of dark Belgian chocolate.

name: Espresso
descr: A concentrated shot of rich, full-bodied coffee with a golden crema on top.

name: Americano
descr: Espresso shots topped with hot water, creating a smooth and balanced flavor profile.

name: Cortado
descr: Equal parts espresso and warm, silky steamed milk. Perfectly balanced and strong.

name: Flat White
descr: A velvety blend of double espresso and finely textured micro-foam milk.

name: Spanish Latte
descr: A sweet, creamy delight made with espresso, steamed milk, and a touch of condensed milk.

name: Cappuccino
descr: A classic mix of espresso, steamed milk, and a thick layer of airy milk foam.

name: Sourdough
descr: A distinct tangy flavor, a chewy crumb (large air holes), and a thick, blistered crust.

name: Ciabatta
descr: An elongated, broad, and flat shape . It has a thin, floury crust and a very porous interior.

name: Baguette
descr: A long, thin shape with deep diagonal slashes on the top. It should have a crisp, golden-brown exterior and a soft.

name: Focaccia
descr: It has a high olive oil content, making the bottom almost fried and crispy, while the top remains soft and pillowy.

name: Challah
descr: The addition of eggs and oil gives it a golden color and a soft. It is often topped with sesame seeds.

name: Rye and Pumpernickel
descr: They are much denser and darker than wheat-based breads.
